title: Luffy Refuses to Let Krieg Break the Baratie
description: On pages 5–8, the fight against Krieg reaches a moral breaking point as Gin, Sanji, Zeff, and Luffy clash over debt, survival, and strength.
scene: Krieg’s weapons and cruelty force everyone at the Baratie to reveal what they truly value: victory, loyalty, survival, or protecting someone else’s dream.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Aghhh! Are you trying to kill yourself!?

dialogue.speaker: Gin
dialogue.text: Hah! Well, well... ain't that a flashy last stand!?

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: But the time to close up this restaurant is now at hand!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: from now on, it'll be a pirate ship!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The restaurant's going to burn down for sure this time!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: H-Hey! We gotta put out this fire or there'll be no point in taking over this ship!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'm tryin' but the fire's too big!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Feel free to fight back if you want! That is, as long as you don't mind letting the old man die!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Stop you idiot! Don't attack him!

summary: This part pushes the Baratie battle toward its climax by contrasting Krieg’s ruthless power with Luffy and Sanji’s stubborn ideals.
